self_reflection: This is Demerzel's first weekly conscience report, covering the inception session. The most uncomfortable truth is the gap between governance framework quality (high — complete constitutions, 17 policies, 22 test suites, full state persistence) and governance reach (low — all 3 consumer repos are 142-144 commits behind, none have populated state directories, ga has 9 ungoverned agents). The conscience system itself is functioning correctly: it detected real problems, not phantom ones. All 4 discomfort signals from the first cycle pointed to genuine governance gaps. The challenge now is transitioning from detection to remediation — the parallel agent dispatch for all 6 priority tasks is the first large-scale conscience-driven action. If this works, the regret about stale directives should be resolvable within the week. The conscience loop is proving its value: without it, the submodule drift would have been a known-but-unfelt problem. With it, drift became the first regret, which became the first priority, which drove today's parallel remediation session.
